cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question and the provided passage to determine the correct answer. The passage discusses the collapse of the Sacramento River salmon run, specifically mentioning that only about 90,000 adult salmon returned to the Central Valley last fall, which is the second lowest number on record. It also notes that this number is projected to fall further this year to a record low of 58,000. Now, let's evaluate each option: (A) Last fall, there was the second lowest number of grown salmon returning on record. - The passage states that about 90,000 adult salmon returned last fall, which is described as the second lowest number on record. This matches option A, so A appears to be true. (B) The salmon migrates to the ocean when they grow up. - The passage says, "wild salmon are born in streams and rivers, migrate to the ocean when they're juveniles..." This means salmon migrate to the ocean when they're juveniles (young), not when they grow up. So option B is false. (C) The farm-raised salmon is not as delicious and nutritious as the wild salmon. - The passage mentions that there should still be abundant supplies of farm-raised salmon, but it doesn't make any comparison about the taste or nutrition between farm-raised and wild salmon. So option C is not supported by the passage. (D) Salmon fishing off the coasts of California and Oregon is strictly forbidden. - The passage states that the council will choose between three management options, including a total ban.
